problem_statement: As software systems grow complex, ensuring security is hard: manual audits are slow and expensive while automated tools have high false-positive rates. Bugdar addresses how to provide near real-time, context-aware secure code review of GitHub pull requests using LLMs.
magnitude_evidence: In the DeFi sector, manual audits often take weeks and cost 'tens of thousands of dollars per project'; internally a 10,000-line Move contract took two security engineers 10 days (0.11 lines/sec) and not all vulnerabilities were found.
insufficiency_evidence: GPT-4o classification reached only 35% precision / 60% recall (F1 0.44) with high false-positive rate; o1-preview 24% precision; and static tools 'overlooked' a reentrancy bug — while Bugdar itself missed a logic flaw and misflagged a safe Rust 'unsafe' block.
